response.justification: Sarah is a talented full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship fundamentals and relevant IDE tooling experience at National Instruments. However, she is critically mismatched for this role—her career has been predominantly TypeScript/Node.js focused with minimal Java experience and zero Spring or Kotlin expertise. For a mid-senior IDE/JVM tooling position requiring end-to-end ownership of enterprise frameworks and application server integration, the absence of deep Java/JVM domain knowledge and Spring ecosystem experience is disqualifying.
strengths: Proven ability to design clean, extensible systems across complex distributed platforms (micro-services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second)
Prior IDE development experience at National Instruments with C IDE (LabWindows/CVI) and current developer tooling work with MCP code generators
Senior-level software craftsmanship with strong commitment to TDD, CI/CD, and teaching others—evidenced by multiple staff roles and workshop leadership
concerns: Severely limited Java experience (only brief mentions at Altom and National Instruments); no Kotlin expertise despite both being core requirements
Zero Spring or enterprise Java framework experience; career trajectory is TypeScript/Node.js, D, and web platforms—not JVM-focused
Last significant Java work was 2014–2018; recent 7+ years concentrated on non-JVM ecosystems, indicating substantial ramp-up needed for mid-senior IC ownership

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
